summary: The formation of multiple roots in multirooted teeth occurs at the bell stage when the dental lamina breaks down, enabling independent root formations. This process involves no involvement with options like the epithelial diaphragm or cell rests of Malassez that are more associated with other aspects of dentition biology but do not cause multiplicity within single developing structures as seen here.
